title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Verification
verification_method: Planned requirement-home execution, negative transition tests, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase and epoch transitions, BenchmarkDotNet coverage, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.
scope: Verify the future repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle for RFC 9001 Section 6 and Sections 6.1 through 6.6, including local and peer initiated updates, repeated Key Phase alternation, successor packet opening, duplicate and tampered transition rejection, current-phase acknowledgment gating, AEAD usage-limit update requests, old-key retention, and old-key discard synchronized with sender/recovery state. The current artifact records the bounded first-successor retention and discard floors, the helper-backed AEAD protected/opened-packet counter, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification floors, bounded runtime first-update/repeated-update-or-discard send/open wiring, bounded repeated local install after current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment plus prior old-key discard, bounded retained phase-1 old-key discard cleanup after local or peer phase-2 install, bounded local phase-3 install after phase-2 acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded peer phase-3 packet opening, phase-3 lower-recovered retained phase-2 receive selection, phase-2 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-2 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-3 install, bounded phase-4 local install and peer opening after phase-3 confirmation, phase-4 lower/higher recovered selection, phase-3 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-3 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-4 install, bounded phase-5 local install and peer opening after phase-4 confirmation, phase-5 lower/higher recovered selection, phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-4 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-5 install, representative phases 6 through 16 property/fuzz coverage for the repeated local-update gate, peer next-key opening, old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, and retained-previous cleanup shape, bounded repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local updates after the repeated-update gate opens, bounded endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset-only emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, and bounded retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard. It does not claim broader stateless-reset response behavior outside that retained-route AEAD-limit floor or a complete unbounded epoch ledger.
preconditions: REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0008 remain the baseline RFC 9001 Section 6 requirements for QUIC Key Phase updates.
REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 define the promoted lifecycle, retention, ordering, and AEAD-limit policy boundary for implementation.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0145 remains the implemented first-successor install prerequisite.
The 9001-06-1rtt-key-update-lifecycle gap remains open for broader repeated epoch-ledger cleanup and stateless-reset response behavior outside the bounded AEAD-limit retained-route floor.
procedure: Run the existing first-successor RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests as a regression baseline.
Run the direct RFC 9001 Section 6.1 first-successor requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010.
Run the direct first RFC 9001 Section 6.2 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004.
Run the direct invalid Key Phase RFC 9001 Section 6.3 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001.
Run the direct first next-receive-key RFC 9001 Section 6.3 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002.
Run the direct first send-key ordering RFC 9001 Section 6.4 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003.
Run the direct first old/current next receive-selection RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002.
Run the direct bounded repeated lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive-selection tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001.
Run the direct first retained-old timer and discard RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
Run the direct first repeated local-update cooldown RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.
Run the direct bounded repeated local install and outbound Key Phase parity tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.

Run the direct bounded repeated peer second-successor tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002.
Run the direct bounded repeated old-key discard cleanup tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 after local and peer phase-2 installs.

Run the direct retained-old cleanup state-isolation tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 to prove old-key expiry preserves current-phase acknowledgment and cooldown state while still removing retained old material and matching sender/recovery state.

Run the bounded phase-3 local install, retained phase-2 lower-recovered selection, retained phase-2 old-key ACK parity, and retained phase-2 cleanup tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.

Run the bounded phase-4 local install, peer phase-4 opening and ACK protection, retained phase-3 lower-recovered selection, phase-4 higher-recovered next-key selection, retained phase-3 old-key ACK parity, and retained phase-3 cleanup tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.

Run the bounded phase-5 local install, peer phase-5 opening and ACK protection, retained phase-4 lower-recovered selection, phase-5 higher-recovered next-key selection, retained phase-4 old-key ACK parity, and retained phase-4 cleanup tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
Run representative later-phase property/fuzz coverage for phases 6 through 16 without treating it as a complete unbounded epoch ledger.
Run the optional `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003` consecutive peer-update policy proof without creating a distinguishable invalid-Key-Phase signal.
Run the direct helper-backed RFC 9001 Section 6.6 AEAD key lifecycle requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` and `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0002`.
Run the direct helper-backed RFC 9001 Section 6.6 AEAD limit policy tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0003` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005`.
Run the direct endpoint helper-backed RFC 9001 Section 6.6 stateless-reset-only emission tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005` after AEAD-limit terminal discard.
Run the direct connected endpoint-host and listener-host RFC 9001 Section 6.6 retained-route stateless-reset response tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005` after AEAD-limit terminal discard.

Add and run positive tests for repeated local-initiated and peer-initiated key updates that alternate the Key Phase bit across more than one epoch only after the confirmation/timing/AEAD-policy subset is in place.

Add and run negative tests for duplicate same-phase packets, stale older-epoch packets, tampered successor packets, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, unacknowledged repeated local updates, premature old-key discard, and authentication failure rollback.

Run the bounded runtime wiring tests that prove AEAD usage-limit decisions install the first local key update before excess protection, discard when successor protection cannot continue, and discard receive paths when integrity limits are reached without sending close datagrams.
Add fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation, packet-protection epoch transition boundaries, and endpoint stateless-reset gate combinations.
Run permanent BenchmarkDotNet suites for successor derivation, epoch install, protected packet formatting, opening in the repeated lifecycle path, and stateless-reset helper paths.
Render SpecTrace Markdown from JSON and run the repo-local trace validation checks available for the touched artifacts.
expected_result: The eventual lifecycle proof shows that endpoints can repeatedly update 1-RTT packet-protection keys after handshake confirmation, use the Key Phase bit to identify and detect the active epoch, decrypt packets that carry a changed Key Phase, keep both endpoints synchronized, gate local repeated updates on current-phase acknowledgment, handle AEAD usage limits without bypassing that gate, retain old keys long enough for safe reordering, reject packet-number-incoherent old-key packets where required, and discard old 1-RTT material only with sender/recovery cleanup. Until then, this verification remains planned and the repository only claims the bounded first-successor floor, including the first retained-old timer and discard cleanup slice, plus helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded first-update/repeated-update-or-discard runtime wiring, bounded repeated local install/send parity after current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment plus prior old-key discard, bounded retained phase-1 old-key discard cleanup after local or peer phase-2 install, bounded local phase-3 install after phase-2 acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded peer phase-3 packet opening, phase-3 lower-recovered retained phase-2 selection, phase-2 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-2 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-3 install, bounded local phase-4 install after phase-3 acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded peer phase-4 packet opening and ACK protection, phase-4 lower/higher recovered selection, phase-3 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-3 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-4 install, bounded local phase-5 install after phase-4 acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded peer phase-5 packet opening and ACK protection, phase-5 lower/higher recovered selection, phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, bounded retained phase-4 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-5 install, representative phases 6 through 16 property/fuzz coverage for local install, peer next-key opening, old-key ACK error, and retained-previous cleanup shape, bounded repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local update after the repeated-update gate opens, bounded endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset-only emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, and bounded retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard. Broader stateless-reset response behavior and a complete unbounded epoch ledger remain unclaimed.
status_summary: Planned for the broader repeated lifecycle. The current evidence set through 2026-04-24 proves the bounded first-successor, repeated phase-2, bounded local phase-3, bounded phase-4, bounded phase-5 topoff, representative phases 6 through 16 lifecycle-shape coverage, AEAD-limit runtime, endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset-only emission, and retained-route host stateless-reset response floors, while keeping broader stateless-reset response behavior open. The representative multi-epoch proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 with 53 focused tests, a 177-test S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-multi-epoch/. The 2026-04-24 representative later-epoch class run passed 18 focused tests and is preserved under artifacts/verification/20260424-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-later-epoch/focused-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-tests.log. Earlier detailed per-slice evidence remains listed in the evidence ledger. The touched Markdown views render cleanly; repo-wide SpecTrace core validation still fails at the standing residual baseline outside this slice. The next missing proof set is a real unbounded repeated epoch-ledger cleanup model and stateless-reset behavior outside the bounded AEAD-limit retained-route floor.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Evidence Boundary
supplemental_sections.content:
- Current tests prove first-successor install, changed-Key Phase observation, tampered-packet rejection, duplicate same-phase rejection, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, first current-phase acknowledgment tracking, the first three-PTO post-confirmation repeated local-update cooldown, bounded repeated local install after cooldown and retained-old discard, outbound Key Phase parity for phase 2, bounded local phase-3, phase-4, and phase-5 installs after acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, representative phases 6 through 16 local update installation, peer next-key opening, old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, and retained-previous cleanup, bounded peer phase-3, phase-4, and phase-5 packet opening, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-2 receive selection after local phase-3 install, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-3 receive selection after local phase-4 install, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-4 receive selection after local phase-5 install, bounded phase-4 and phase-5 higher-recovered next-key selection, bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment and old-key discard, bounded retained phase-1 old-key discard cleanup after local or peer phase-2 install, bounded retained phase-2 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-3 install, bounded retained phase-3 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-4 install, bounded retained phase-4 old-key discard cleanup after local phase-5 install, per-key-phase current packet-number floor isolation, timing-neutral invalid Key Phase drop classification, timing-neutral no-close handling for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, the first retained-old discard timer after authenticating a new-key packet, the first old 1-RTT read-key discard floor with matching sender/recovery cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, next-Key-Phase guard for successor install, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, phase-2, phase-3, and phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity for newer-key sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded runtime first-update/repeated-update-or-discard send/open wiring, endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset-only emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, automatic retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets.
- Current benchmarks cover the first-successor install, repeated local install, repeated peer install, retained phase-1 packet opening after repeated local update, protected packet formatting/opening path, repeated local-update control-state checks, repeated phase-1 and phase-2 old-key cleanup state, helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle counter/limit, repeated-update gate, policy-decision checks, and stateless-reset helper plus retained-route lookup paths.
- No evidence in this artifact claims broader stateless-reset response behavior or a complete unbounded repeated 1-RTT epoch-ledger cleanup model.

supplemental_sections.heading: Traffic Secret Advancement Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 traffic-secret advancement run proves that a committed first update advances stored application traffic secrets to the updated `quic ku` secrets and that the helper can derive the next successor packet keys from those advanced secrets.
- The same run preserved the then-current repeated-local-update negative boundary: second-successor material derivation was possible, but repeated local update installation still failed before the current-phase acknowledgment/old-key-discard gate was implemented.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-traffic-secret-advance/` with focused S6P1 tests, the all-S6P1 guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Local Install Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated-local install run proves that a local endpoint can install phase-2 material only after the current phase is acknowledged, the three-PTO cooldown has elapsed, and retained-old first-update material has been discarded.
- The same run proves retained-old material blocks repeated local install, phase-2 protect/open material is installed from advanced traffic secrets, displaced phase-1 material is retained old, and outbound Key Phase emission uses parity so phase 2 writes bit 0.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-local-install/` with focused S6/S6P5 tests, the S6P5 guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Lower-Recovered Receive Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated lower-recovered receive-selection run proves that, after bounded local phase-2 install, the runtime can authenticate lower recovered phase-1 packets using retained previous material.
- The same run proves retained phase-1 material is not accepted when the packet carries the phase-0 bit, isolates the current packet-number floor per installed key phase, and keeps phase-2 current-packet observation from poisoning valid lower recovered phase-1 selection.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-lower-recovered-selection/` with focused S6P5 tests, the repeated receive-selection guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Peer Second-Successor Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated peer second-successor run proves that, after current-phase acknowledgment and prior old-key discard, the runtime can retain second-successor receive material and authenticate peer phase-2 packets with Key Phase bit 0.
- The same run proves phase-2 send keys are installed before ACK emission, repeated higher recovered packet numbers use second-successor keys, and apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates remain timing-neutral drops.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-repeated-peer-second-successor/` with focused S6P2/S6P3/S6P5 tests, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Old-Key Discard Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated old-key discard run proves retained phase-1 cleanup after local and peer phase-2 installs for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
- The same run proves the retained phase-1 timer does not extend on later phase-2 packets, phase-1 read material is discarded on expiry, matching phase-1 sender/recovery state is removed, phase-2 state remains intact, and representative randomized packet sets keep cleanup scoped to phase 1.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-old-key-discard/` with 15 focused S6P5 tests, a 123-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Phase-3 Epoch-Ledger Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 bounded phase-3 run proves local phase-3 install after phase-2 acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old discard for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.
- The same run proves bounded peer phase-3 packet opening after phase-2 confirmation for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, phase-2 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, retained phase-2 lower recovered receive selection for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, retained phase-2 timer arming for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004, and retained phase-2 cleanup with matching sender/recovery state for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-phase3-epoch-ledger/` with 51 focused phase-3 tests, a 145-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Phase-4 Epoch-Ledger Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 bounded phase-4 run proves local phase-4 install after phase-3 acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old discard for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.
- The same run proves bounded peer phase-4 packet opening and ACK-key update after phase-3 confirmation for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, phase-3 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, retained phase-3 lower recovered receive selection for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, phase-4 higher recovered next-key selection for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002, retained phase-3 timer arming for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004, and retained phase-3 cleanup with matching sender/recovery state for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-phase4-epoch-ledger/` with 75 focused phase-4 tests, a 159-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Phase-5 Epoch-Ledger Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 bounded phase-5 run proves local phase-5 install after phase-4 acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old discard for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.
- The same run proves bounded peer phase-5 packet opening and ACK-key update after phase-4 confirmation for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, retained phase-4 lower recovered receive selection for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, phase-5 higher recovered next-key selection for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002, retained phase-4 timer arming for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004, and retained phase-4 cleanup with matching sender/recovery state for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-phase5-epoch-ledger/` with 89 focused phase-5 tests, a 173-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Representative Multi-Epoch Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 representative multi-epoch run proves phases 6 through 16 are not hard-coded to the bounded phase-5 topoff for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
- The same run proves later local installs still require current-phase acknowledgment, cooldown, and prior retained-old discard; later peer updates open with next keys after confirmation and prior old-key discard; later retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge newer sends close with KEY_UPDATE_ERROR; and later retained-previous cleanup drops only the retained previous phase while preserving current state across representative packet sets.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-multi-epoch/` with 53 focused tests, a 177-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation. This remains representative helper-backed proof, not a complete unbounded epoch ledger.
- The 2026-04-24 representative later-epoch class run extends the sampled later-phase shape through the same phases 6 through 16 boundary, passed 18 focused tests, and is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260424-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-later-epoch/focused-rfc9001-s6p5-representative-tests.log`.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated AEAD-Limit Local-Update Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated AEAD-limit local-update run proves the send path can install a bounded repeated local key update before protecting beyond an exhausted current 1-RTT confidentiality limit, but only after the current phase is acknowledged, the repeated cooldown has elapsed, and retained old material has been discarded.
- The same run preserves the stop-connection behavior when a key update is impossible and adds randomized policy coverage that maps the repeated-update gate to update-required versus stop-connection decisions.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p6-repeated-aead-limit-update/` with 17 focused S6P6 tests, a 125-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded AEAD-Limit Stateless-Reset Endpoint Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 endpoint helper-backed reset-only run proves that AEAD-limit terminal discard preserves enough endpoint state for `TryCreateStatelessResetDatagram` to emit a stateless reset only when an existing token is registered.
- The same run proves no reset is fabricated without a token, no close datagram is emitted by the discard transition, and loop-prevention plus rate-limit gates still apply after terminal discard.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p6-aead-limit-stateless-reset-endpoint/` with 9 focused S6P6-0005 tests, a 128-test S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded AEAD-Limit Host Stateless-Reset Response Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 host reset response run proves that AEAD-limit terminal discard preserves enough token-linked retained route and path state for direct endpoint lookup, the connected endpoint host, and the listener host to emit a stateless reset for a later packet whose destination connection ID resolves to that retained route.
- The same run proves unlinked routes do not fabricate resets, retained routes are scoped to the same remote address and port, loop-prevention and rate-limit gates remain active, and no connection-close datagram is emitted by the discard transition.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p6-aead-limit-host-stateless-reset-response/` with 15 focused S6P6-0005 tests, an S6P1-through-S6P6 guard, and a QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Next Planned Proof Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Keep broader stateless-reset response behavior outside the bounded AEAD-limit retained-route floor blocked until its response matrix, packet-to-token lookup boundary, loop prevention, rate gating, and proof plan are traced.
- Continue repeated key-update work with a real unbounded epoch-ledger and cleanup ownership model beyond the representative later-phase proof.
